message:
fix: replace expect() with proper error handling in schema cache and egress

Two improvements to error handling that prevent panics and improve clarity:

1. crates/fcp-graphql/src/schema.rs - Schema cache lock poisoning
   - Replace .expect("schema cache lock") with proper Result propagation
   - Return GraphqlClientError::Protocol on poisoned mutex instead of panic
   - Drop the guard explicitly before expensive operations to minimize hold time
   - Fixes potential panic in multi-threaded GraphQL clients

2. crates/fcp-sandbox/src/egress.rs - Hostname canonicalization logic
   - Separate the "hostname changed" check from "require canonicalization" check
   - When canonicalization is required AND hostname differs: return error
   - When canonicalization NOT required AND hostname differs: just log debug
   - Previously, the require_host_canonicalization check was nested incorrectly
     and could only trigger when the hostname was already canonical
   - Now properly enforces the canonicalization requirement

Both changes improve robustness of the FCP connector ecosystem:
- GraphQL clients won't panic on lock contention
- Egress policy enforcement works as documented

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
